explanation: This command uses Dsacls.exe to grant full access (GA) to a user, matching the Sigma rule's detection criteria. The command includes safe mutations like quotes around the path and user, and uses the /I:S flag to specify inheritance behavior.
This command grants extended access (GE) to a user. It omits unnecessary quotes and uses short syntax for the /I flag, which is still valid and matches the Sigma rule's detection logic.
This command uses the equivalent syntax of '-G' instead of '/G' to grant read access (GR). The reordering of the path and user arguments is valid for Dsacls.exe and still matches the Sigma rule.
This command grants write access (GW) to a user. It omits optional flags like /I:S to demonstrate that the Sigma rule can still detect the core malicious behavior.
